content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges:
[(2, 9, 2), (5, 7, 2), (0, 5, 4), (5, 6, 4), (1, 2, 6), (1, 5, 6), (5, 9, 6), (2, 7, 8), (2, 6, 10), (6, 8, 12), (7, 9, 14), (6, 7, 15), (2, 5, 16), (3, 4, 16), (1, 9, 17), (3, 8, 17), (2, 4, 19)],

We need to find the nodes linked with node 7 exactly at time 2.

After scanning each edge, we can collect the other nodes as follows:

* Edge (2, 9, 2) has node 2 and time 2. -> Add node 9.
* Edge (5, 7, 2) has node 5 and time 2. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (0, 5, 4) has node 0 and time 4 (not 2). -> Ignore.
* Edge (5, 6, 4) has node 5 and time 4 (not 2). -> Ignore.
* Edge (1, 2, 6) has node 1 and time 2. -> Add node 2.
* Edge (1, 5, 6) has node 1 and time 2. -> Add node 5.
* Edge (5, 9, 6) has node 5 and time 6 (not 2). -> Ignore.
* Edge (2, 7, 8) has node 2 and time 8 (not 2). -> Ignore.
* Edge (2, 6, 10) has node 2 and time 10 (not 2). -> Ignore.
* Edge (6, 8, 12) has node 6 and time 12 (not 2). -> Ignore.
* Edge (7, 9, 14) has node 7 and time 14 (not 2). -> Ignore.
* Edge (6, 7, 15) has node 6 and time 15 (not 2). -> Ignore.
* Edge (2, 5, 16) has node 2 and time 16 (not 2). -> Ignore.
* Edge (3, 4, 16) has node 3 and time 16 (not 2). -> Add node 4.
* Edge (1, 9, 17) has node 1 and time 17 (not 2). -> Add node 9.
* Edge (3, 8, 17) has node 3 and time 17 (not 2). -> Add node 8.
* Edge (2, 4, 19) has node 2 and time 19 (not 2). -> Ignore.

The nodes matched are 9, 7, and 8.

Answer: [9, 7, 8]
